title: QK norm
summary: Normalization applied to query and key vectors before attention scores are computed, stabilizing dot-product magnitudes in deep stacks.
openingSummary: QK norm rescales query and key projections—often with RMSNorm or layer norm—right before the attention dot product so logits stay well behaved as models scale depth or context.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: QK norm is a normalization variant placed on attention queries and keys before their dot product, not on the full residual stream around a sublayer. Implementations typically wrap the projected Q and K tensors with RMSNorm or layer norm while leaving values untouched. It targets score magnitudes inside the attention module rather than replacing block-level pre-norm around attention and the FFN.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Recent large models add QK norm to curb exploding attention logits without changing the outer pre-norm block layout readers already know. Separating QK norm from layer norm clarifies config fields: one knob rescales hidden states entering sublayers, another stabilizes the Q·K interaction itself.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: A multi-head block projects hidden states into queries and keys, applies RMSNorm on each head's Q and K vectors, then computes scaled dot-product scores. Values feed the weighted sum unchanged; only the compatibility scores see the extra normalization step.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: QK norm is not the same as pre-norm around the attention sublayer—it runs inside attention on Q and K only. It also does not replace RMSNorm on the residual stream; many models use both. Finally, QK norm is not rotary position embedding: RoPE encodes position in Q and K, while QK norm rescales their magnitudes.
